subject: Science
overall_learning_objectives: Critically analyze the electrochemical mechanism of nerve impulse conduction and synaptic delay
Evaluate the hierarchical organization of the CNS and PNS in processing complex stimuli
Synthesize the biochemical role of phytohormones in differential growth and apical dominance
Analyze the hypothalamus-pituitary axis and the precision of multi-level feedback loops
Differentiate between electrical and chemical coordination based on speed, range, and persistence
Solve high-order thinking (HOTS) problems on neuro-hormonal integration and physiological disorders

lesson_plan.title: Neurophysiology: The Action Potential
topics_covered: Bio-electric nature of the nerve impulse
Polarization, Depolarization, and Repolarization (Simplified)
The Synaptic cleft: Neurotransmitters and receptor binding
teaching_strategy: Analyzing the 'All-or-None' law of nerve conduction
Graphical representation of impulse transmission speed
Molecular view of the Sodium-Potassium pump (Introductory)
student_activities: Sketch the ionic changes across an axon membrane during an impulse
Research: Why do nerve impulses travel faster in myelinated neurons?
assessment: Worksheet on the sequence of synaptic transmission
HOTS: Why can an impulse only jump the synapse in one direction?

lesson_plan.title: Complex Reflexes and Neural Integration
topics_covered: Cerebral vs. Spinal reflexes
Role of Relay Neurons in the white and grey matter of the Spinal Cord
Conditioned vs. Unconditioned reflexes (Pavlovian intro)
teaching_strategy: Case study: Coordination in professional athletes vs. basic reflexes
Comparative anatomy: Protection of brain vs. protection of spinal cord
Detailed anatomy of the Reflex Arc in 3D perspective
student_activities: Trace the neural pathway for a complex reflex like 'Catching a falling glass'
Differentiate between 'Thoughtful' actions and 'Reflexive' actions in a T-chart
assessment: Labeling the internal structure of the Spinal Cord
Reasoning: Why is the reflex arc more efficient for survival than conscious thought?

lesson_plan.title: Advanced Neuroanatomy
topics_covered: The Cerebrum: Sensory, Motor, and Association areas
Hypothalamus: The link between Nervous and Endocrine systems
Involuntary centers of the Medulla: Cardiovascular and Respiratory
teaching_strategy: Analyzing brain lesions and their effect on behavior (Case study approach)
Discussion on how the Cerebellum processes 'Muscle Memory'
The role of the Blood-Brain Barrier (Conceptual)
student_activities: Map the specific lobes of the brain to complex tasks (e.g., decision making, visual processing)
Debate: Does the brain ever truly 'sleep'? (Center activity during REM)
assessment: Identification test based on clinical symptoms (e.g., 'If a person loses balance, which part is affected?')

lesson_plan.title: Phytohormone Kinetics and Tropisms
topics_covered: Auxin concentration gradients and Apical Dominance
Gibberellins and Bolting; Cytokinins and Senescence
Thigmotropism: Mechanism of tendril coiling
teaching_strategy: Analyzing Darwin’s experiment on Phototropism
Mathematical logic of differential growth in roots vs. shoots
Interactive simulation of plant responses to external stimuli
student_activities: Predict the growth pattern of a decapitated plant shoot
Design an experiment to prove Hydrotropism is stronger than Geotropism
assessment: Short answer: Why is Abscisic acid called the 'Stress Hormone'?

lesson_plan.title: The Endocrine Axis
topics_covered: The Hypothalamus-Pituitary-Target Gland axis
Trophic hormones and their regulatory roles
Synergistic and Antagonistic hormonal actions (Insulin vs. Glucagon)
teaching_strategy: Flowchart of the master gland's control over the Thyroid and Adrenals
Analyzing why hormones are required in 'trace amounts'
Discussion on why endocrine signaling is more persistent than neural signaling
student_activities: Draw a complex cascade of hormonal release from Hypothalamus to Peripheral gland
Research the role of the Pineal gland and Melatonin in circadian rhythms

lesson_plan.title: Feedback Loops and Pathophysiology
topics_covered: Negative vs. Positive feedback loops
Endocrine disorders: Gigantism, Acromegaly, Goitre, Diabetes mellitus
The role of Iodine and the biochemical synthesis of Thyroxine
teaching_strategy: Analyzing patient case files for endocrine symptoms
Logic of 'Feedback Inhibition' using the Thermostat model
Comparison: Type 1 vs. Type 2 Diabetes (Simplified)
student_activities: Construct a feedback loop for the regulation of Thyroxine
Identify the specific gland and hormone missing in a set of deficiency symptoms

lesson_plan.title: Integration and Board Excellence
topics_covered: Neuro-endocrine integration recap
Solving NCERT Exemplar and HOTS questions
Board-pattern assertion-reasoning and passage-based questions
teaching_strategy: Mind-mapping the 'Control and Coordination' network
Solving integrated questions (e.g., 'How do nervous and chemical systems work together during a race?')
Reviewing common mistakes in labeling complex diagrams
student_activities: Participate in a peer-review session of descriptive answers
assessment: Detailed feedback on logic and scientific terminology

teaching_aids: NCERT Exemplar Science and high-level reference material
Charts of Feedback Mechanisms and Reflex Arcs
Microscope slides of Spinal Cord cross-section
teacher_tip: For Hard-level students, explain that the 'Brain' doesn't just receive signals; it 'filters' them. Mention that the Thalamus acts as a relay station to help them understand why we aren't constantly aware of the feel of our clothes on our skin.
